title: Galphimia langlassei
taxonomy: Galphimia langlassei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is a member of the genus Galphimia, which comprises plants native to Mexico and Central America. The plant belongs to the order Malpighiales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. The family Malpighiaceae is a group of small shrubs and trees commonly known as Barbados gooseberry or acacia family. Galphimia langlassei is a relatively rare species found in specific regions.

Its extracts have been studied for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation.
